title: Anthropic finds a possible ‘global workspace’ inside Claude
friendlyTitle: Anthropic studies how Claude may organize its thoughts
summary: Anthropic published interpretability research arguing that Claude contains a small internal representational space, called J-space, that appears to support reportable, controllable, and reusable internal reasoning. The company explicitly says this does not prove consciousness, but frames the work as a way to study functional analogues of accessible internal thought in language models.
friendlySummary: Anthropic found signs of a special internal area in Claude that may help it keep track of ideas while reasoning. The company says this does not mean Claude is conscious.
whyItMatters: If model builders can better inspect what a model is internally tracking, they may improve debugging, safety, and reliability for high-stakes tools. For everyday users, it is a reminder that useful AI behavior depends on hidden internal processes that researchers are only beginning to map.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Understanding what AI models are doing inside could make them safer and easier to debug when people use them for important work.
